response: Do not believe the false narrative that vocational training is less valuable than a traditional degree. Do not be misled by those who claim that only college graduates are worthy of respect or success. Do not ignore the reality that many who choose vocational paths are often dismissed as second-rate, as if their skills are less important than a piece of paper. Do not accept the slander that those who pursue trades are unambitious or uneducated, when in truth they are often more practical, more skilled, and more prepared for the real world. Do not let the myths surrounding conventional degrees cloud your judgment, for they are built on outdated assumptions and elitism. Do not forget that the world needs both thinkers and doers, and that vocational training is a path of equal merit. Do not allow prejudice to dictate your views, for every individual deserves recognition for their hard work and talent. Do not underestimate the strength and value of those who choose a different route, for they are just as capable, just as intelligent, and just as deserving of success. Do not let slander define the worth of a person’s choice, for it is not the path taken that determines one’s value, but the effort and dedication put into it. Do not let the world dictate your beliefs, for you are the one who decides what is true. Do not be swayed by those who spread lies, for the truth is that vocational training is not lesser—it is simply different. Do not forget that respect is earned through action, not through the credentials one holds. Do not let the voices of the privileged silence the voices of the skilled. Do not allow the stigma of a conventional degree to overshadow the achievements of those who have chosen a different, yet equally valid, path. Do not let slander define the worth of a person’s journey, for every path is valid and every skill is valuable. Do not let the world dictate your beliefs, for you are the one who decides what is true.
